description_long: This is a wooden ancient-style boat with a single large white sail featuring a blue Greek key pattern and a central emblem. The hull is elongated and narrow, constructed from interlocking wooden planks, and both the bow and stern curve upward in a classical design. The deck is open, with visible wooden slats and no covered areas, allowing for passengers or cargo to be placed inside. The mast is centrally positioned, supporting the sail with a horizontal yard at the top. The boat lacks any visible modern propulsion, relying on the sail for movement, and there are no oarlocks or motor mounts present. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a Greek or Roman trireme, suitable for historical or decorative use in an urban park or waterfront setting.
description: A narrow, wooden ancient-style boat with a large white sail featuring a blue Greek motif and emblem. The hull is open and curved at both ends, with a central mast and no visible modern propulsion.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed bow, upward-curved hull, and the full face of the decorated sail.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated hull, upward-curved bow and stern, and the profile of the mast and sail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the stern's upward curve, the back of the open deck, and the reverse side of the sail's emblem.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same elongated hull and sail profile, with the mast and sail visible from the other side.
